revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''切山椒'''（きりざんしょう）は、[[和菓子]]の一種で、[[正月]]に食される[[餅|餅菓子]]である。[[糝粉]]に砂糖と...」
revision.text:
'''切山椒'''（きりざんしょう）は、[[和菓子]]の一種で、[[正月]]に食される[[餅|餅菓子]]である。[[糝粉]]に砂糖と[[山椒]]を炒った粉をまぜて（または、その粉をぬるま湯にしみ出させたものをまぜて）練った生地を蒸し、[[臼]]で搗いたのち、[[算木]]型ないし[[拍子木]]形（細長い直方体）に切りそろえて作る<ref name="沢">沢史生 [[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 切山椒]」 日本大百科全書、2016年7月25日閲覧</ref><ref name="山本"/>。色は[[紅白]]に染めたものが多いが、[[碾茶]]や[[黒糖]]などを使い五色にしたものもある<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998年、1328-1329頁。</ref>。臼で搗いたのちさらに蒸して搗き直したものは'''練山椒'''（ねりざんしょう）といい、極上品の[[ぎゅうひ]]菓子として扱われる<ref name="沢"/>。
[[江戸時代]]からある菓子で、茶人の[[小堀遠州]]が好んだと伝えられる<ref name="沢"/>。古くは山椒餅とも呼ばれた。算木型になったのは江戸後期から言われている<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997年、71-72頁。</ref>。
[[俳諧]]では新年の[[季語]]としても扱われる<ref name="春樹"/>。 {{quote|わかくさのいろも添へたり切山椒 [[久保田万太郎]]}} <!--歳時記ではないので、過度に列挙されぬよう-->
== 出典 == <references/>
{{food-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きりさんしよう}} [[Category:餅菓子]] [[Category:正月]] [[Category:行事食]] [[Category:新春の季語]]

revision.comment: 以前からこの項目がなぜないのかと思っていました

revision.text:
[[File:Kirizansho.jpg|250px|thumb|切山椒]] '''切山椒'''（きりざんしょう）は、[[和菓子]]の一種で、[[正月]]に食される[[餅|餅菓子]]である。[[糝粉]]に砂糖と[[山椒]]を炒った粉をまぜて（または、その粉をぬるま湯にしみ出させたものをまぜて）練った生地を蒸し、[[臼]]で搗いたのち、[[算木]]形ないし[[拍子木]]形（細長い直方体）に切りそろえて作る<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 切山椒]」 日本大百科全書、2016年7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>。色は[[紅白]]に染めたものが多いが、[[碾茶]]や[[黒糖]]などを使い五色にしたものもある<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998年、1328-1329頁。</ref>。臼で搗いたのちさらに蒸して搗き直したものは'''練山椒'''（ねりざんしょう）といい、極上品の[[ぎゅうひ]]菓子として扱われる<ref name="沢"/>。
[[江戸時代]]からある菓子で、茶人の[[小堀遠州]]が好んだと伝えられる<ref name="沢"/>。古くは山椒餅とも呼ばれた。算木形になったのは江戸後期からと言われている<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997年、71-72頁。</ref>。また、[[酉の市]]で売られる縁起物でもある<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>。
[[俳諧]]では新年の[[季語]]としても扱われる<ref name="春樹"/>。 {{quote|わかくさのいろも添へたり切山椒 [[久保田万太郎]]}} <!--歳時記ではないので、過度に列挙されぬよう-->
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
{{food-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きりさんしよう}} [[Category:餅菓子]] [[Category:正月]] [[Category:ハレの食事]] [[Category:新春の季語]]

[[File:Kirizansho.jpg|250px|thumb|切山椒]] '''切山椒'''（きりざんしょう）は、[[和菓子]]の一種で、[[正月]]に食される[[餅|餅菓子]]である。[[糝粉]]に砂糖と[[山椒]]を炒った粉をまぜて（または、その粉をぬるま湯にしみ出させたものをまぜて）練った生地を蒸し、[[臼]]で搗いたのち、[[算木]]形ないし[[拍子木]]形（細長い直方体）に切りそろえて作る<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 切山椒]」 日本大百科全書、2016年7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>。色は[[紅白]]に染めたものが多いが、[[碾茶]]や[[黒糖]]などを使い五色にしたものもある<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998年、1328-1329頁。</ref>。臼で搗いたのちさらに蒸して搗き直したものは'''練山椒'''（ねりざんしょう）といい、極上品の[[ぎゅうひ]]菓子として扱われる<ref name="沢"/>。
[[江戸時代]]からある菓子で、茶人の[[小堀遠州]]が好んだと伝えられる<ref name="沢"/>。古くは山椒餅とも呼ばれた。算木形になったのは江戸後期からと言われている<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997年、71-72頁。</ref>。また、[[酉の市]]で売られる縁起物でもある<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>。
[[俳諧]]では新年の[[季語]]としても扱われる<ref name="春樹"/>。
山形県鶴岡市に古くから伝わる切り山椒は、毎年12月17日、鶴岡市旧七日町の観音堂のお歳夜に、縁起菓子として売られている。鶴岡の「切山椒」は、こくのある黒糖と山椒の味がきいた細長く切られたもち菓子で、山椒の果皮を乾燥させて粉にし、ふっくらと蒸しあげた餅に混ぜながら練り上げて作っている。鶴岡では厄除けに鬼門の位置にとげがある山椒の木を植える習慣があることから、山椒が使われている「切山椒」は厄を祓い新年に良きことあれと願う年の瀬の風物詩として長らく鶴岡の人々に愛されてきた。 {{quote|わかくさのいろも添へたり切山椒 [[久保田万太郎]]}} <!--歳時記ではないので、過度に列挙されぬよう-->

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/中村恵二|中村恵二]] ([[User talk:中村恵二|会話]]) による ID:72370586 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]] 無出典。木村屋の関係者の方でしょうか

[[File:Kirizansho.jpg|250px|thumb|切山椒]] '''切山椒'''（きりざんしょう）は、[[和菓子]]の一種で、[[正月]]に食される[[餅|餅菓子]]である。[[糝粉]]に砂糖と[[山椒]]を炒った粉をまぜて（または、その粉をぬるま湯にしみ出させたものをまぜて）練った生地を蒸し、[[臼]]で搗いたのち、[[算木]]形ないし[[拍子木]]形（細長い直方体）に切りそろえて作る<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 切山椒]」 日本大百科全書、2016年7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>。色は[[紅白]]に染めたものが多いが、[[碾茶]]や[[黒糖]]などを使い五色にしたものもある<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998年、1328-1329頁。</ref>。臼で搗いたのちさらに蒸して搗き直したものは'''練山椒'''（ねりざんしょう）といい、極上品の[[ぎゅうひ]]菓子として扱われる<ref name="沢"/>。

revision.text:
{{ill|Kirizanshō|ja|切山椒}} is a type of {{ill|wagashi|ja|和菓子}} (Japanese confectionery) traditionally eaten during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}}. It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a {{ill|mortar and pestle|ja|臼}}, and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling {{ill|Sangoku|ja|算木}} or {{ill|hyōshigi|ja|拍子木}} (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016年7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>
The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998年、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>
Kirizanshō dates back to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and is said to have been favored by the tea master {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}}.<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive {{ill|sangoku|ja|算木}} shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997年、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>
In {{ill|haikai|ja|俳諧}}, Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ({{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> {{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō'' [[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}

{{ill|Kirizanshō|ja|切山椒}} is a type of {{ill|wagashi|ja|和菓子}} (Japanese confectionery) traditionally eaten during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}}. It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a {{ill|mortar and pestle|ja|臼}}, and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling {{ill|Sangoku|ja|算木}} or {{ill|hyōshigi|ja|拍子木}} (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016年7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>
The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998年、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>
Kirizanshō dates back to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and is said to have been favored by the tea master {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}}.<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive {{ill|sangoku|ja|算木}} shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997年、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>
In {{ill|haikai|ja|俳諧}}, Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ({{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> {{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō'' [[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}

revision.text:
[[File:Kirizansho.jpg|250px|thumb|切山椒]] '''切山椒'''（きりざんしょう）は、[[和菓子]]の一種で、[[正月]]に食される[[餅|餅菓子]]である。[[糝粉]]に砂糖と[[山椒]]を炒った粉をまぜて（または、その粉をぬるま湯にしみ出させたものをまぜて）練った生地を蒸し、[[臼]]で搗いたのち、[[算木]]形ないし[[拍子木]]形（細長い直方体）に切りそろえて作る<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 切山椒]」 日本大百科全書、2016年7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>。色は[[紅白]]に染めたものが多いが、[[碾茶]]や[[黒糖]]などを使い五色にしたものもある<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998年、1328-1329頁。</ref>。臼で搗いたのちさらに蒸して搗き直したものは'''練山椒'''（ねりざんしょう）といい、極上品の[[ぎゅうひ]]菓子として扱われる<ref name="沢"/>。
[[江戸時代]]からある菓子で、茶人の[[小堀政一|小堀遠州]]が好んだと伝えられる<ref name="沢"/>。古くは山椒餅とも呼ばれた。算木形になったのは江戸後期からと言われている<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997年、71-72頁。</ref>。また、[[酉の市]]で売られる縁起物でもある<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>。
[[俳諧]]では新年の[[季語]]としても扱われる<ref name="春樹"/>。 {{quote|わかくさのいろも添へたり切山椒 [[久保田万太郎]]}} <!--歳時記ではないので、過度に列挙されぬよう-->
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[鶴岡のきりさんしょ]]
{{food-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きりさんしよう}} [[Category:餅菓子]] [[Category:正月]] [[Category:ハレの食事]] [[Category:新春の季語]] [[Category:新年の料理]]

[[File:Kirizansho.jpg|250px|thumb|切山椒]] '''切山椒'''（きりざんしょう）は、[[和菓子]]の一種で、[[正月]]に食される[[餅|餅菓子]]である。[[糝粉]]に砂糖と[[山椒]]を炒った粉をまぜて（または、その粉をぬるま湯にしみ出させたものをまぜて）練った生地を蒸し、[[臼]]で搗いたのち、[[算木]]形ないし[[拍子木]]形（細長い直方体）に切りそろえて作る<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 切山椒]」 日本大百科全書、2016年7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>。色は[[紅白]]に染めたものが多いが、[[碾茶]]や[[黒糖]]などを使い五色にしたものもある<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998年、1328-1329頁。</ref>。臼で搗いたのちさらに蒸して搗き直したものは'''練山椒'''（ねりざんしょう）といい、極上品の[[ぎゅうひ]]菓子として扱われる<ref name="沢"/>。
[[江戸時代]]からある菓子で、茶人の[[小堀政一|小堀遠州]]が好んだと伝えられる<ref name="沢"/>。古くは山椒餅とも呼ばれた。算木形になったのは江戸後期からと言われている<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997年、71-72頁。</ref>。また、[[酉の市]]で売られる縁起物でもある<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>。
[[俳諧]]では新年の[[季語]]としても扱われる<ref name="春樹"/>。 {{quote|わかくさのいろも添へたり切山椒 [[久保田万太郎]]}} <!--歳時記ではないので、過度に列挙されぬよう-->

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28682544]]
Wikidata name: kirizashō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28682544]]
Wikidata name: kirizashō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{ill|Kirizanshō|ja|切山椒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizanshō|qq=|1=kirizashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682544}} is a type of [[:en:Wagashi|wagashi]] (Japanese confectionery) traditionally eaten during the [[:en:Japanese New Year|Japanese New Year]]. It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko (rice flour)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上新粉|1=jōshinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359063}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンショウ|1=Zanthoxylum piperitum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167881}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a [[:en:Usu (mortar)|mortar and pestle]], and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling [[:en:Counting rods|Sangoku]] or [[:en:Hyōshigi|hyōshigi]] (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016th year 7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/> 

The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red and white (Japan)|qq=|1=red and white|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90812259}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matcha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 抹茶|1=matcha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q822331}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokutō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒砂糖|1=black sugar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135455243}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998th year 、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerizanshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gyūhi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 求肥|1=gyūhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109866}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>

Kirizanshō dates back to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and is said to have been favored by the tea master [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]].<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive [[:en:Counting rods|sangoku]] shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>

In [[:en:Haikai|haikai]], Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ([[:en:Kigo|kigo]]) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> 
{{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō''  
[[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}

It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko (rice flour)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上新粉|1=jōshinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359063}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンショウ|1=Zanthoxylum piperitum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167881}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a [[:en:Usu (mortar)|mortar and pestle]], and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling [[:en:Counting rods|Sangoku]] or [[:en:Hyōshigi|hyōshigi]] (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016th year 7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/> 

The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red and white (Japan)|1=red and white|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90812259}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matcha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 抹茶|1=matcha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q822331}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒砂糖|1=black sugar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135455243}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998th year 、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerizanshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gyūhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 求肥|1=gyūhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109866}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>

Kirizanshō dates back to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and is said to have been favored by the tea master [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]].<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive [[:en:Counting rods|sangoku]] shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>

In [[:en:Haikai|haikai]], Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ([[:en:Kigo|kigo]]) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> 
{{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō''  
[[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}

{{ill|Kirizanshō|ja|切山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizanshō|1=kirizashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682544}} is a type of [[:en:Wagashi|wagashi]] (Japanese confectionery) traditionally eaten during the [[:en:Japanese New Year|Japanese New Year]]. It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko (rice flour)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上新粉|1=jōshinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359063}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンショウ|1=Zanthoxylum piperitum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167881}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a [[:en:Usu (mortar)|mortar and pestle]], and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling [[:en:Counting rods|Sangoku]] or [[:en:Hyōshigi|hyōshigi]] (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016th year 7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/> 

The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red and white (Japan)|1=red and white|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90812259}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matcha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 抹茶|1=matcha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q822331}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒砂糖|1=black sugar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135455243}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998th year 、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerizanshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gyūhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 求肥|1=gyūhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109866}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>

Kirizanshō dates back to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and is said to have been favored by the tea master [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]].<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive [[:en:Counting rods|sangoku]] shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>

In [[:en:Haikai|haikai]], Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ([[:en:Kigo|kigo]]) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> 
{{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō''  
[[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28682544]] Wikidata name: kirizashō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28682544]] Wikidata name: kirizashō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Kirizanshō|ja|切山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizanshō|1=kirizashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682544}} is a type of [[:en:Wagashi|wagashi]] (Japanese confectionery) traditionally eaten during the [[:en:Japanese New Year|Japanese New Year]]. It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko (rice flour)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上新粉|1=jōshinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359063}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンショウ|1=Zanthoxylum piperitum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167881}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a [[:en:Usu (mortar)|mortar and pestle]], and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling [[:en:Counting rods|Sangoku]] or [[:en:Hyōshigi|hyōshigi]] (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016th year 7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>
The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red and white (Japan)|1=red and white|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90812259}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matcha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 抹茶|1=matcha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q822331}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒砂糖|1=black sugar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135455243}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998th year 、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerizanshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gyūhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 求肥|1=gyūhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109866}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>
Kirizanshō dates back to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and is said to have been favored by the tea master [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]].<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive [[:en:Counting rods|sangoku]] shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>
In [[:en:Haikai|haikai]], Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ([[:en:Kigo|kigo]]) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> {{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō'' [[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsuruoka no Kirisansho|ja|鶴岡のきりさんしょ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruoka no Kirisansho|1=kirisansho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65293815}}
{{food-stub}}

}}

It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko (rice flour)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上新粉|1=jōshinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359063}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンショウ|1=Zanthoxylum piperitum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167881}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a [[:en:Usu (mortar)|mortar and pestle]], and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling [[:en:Counting rods|Sangoku]] or [[:en:Hyōshigi|hyōshigi]] (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016th year 7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>
The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red and white (Japan)|1=red and white|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90812259}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matcha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 抹茶|1=matcha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q822331}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒砂糖|1=black sugar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135455243}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998th year 、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerizanshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gyūhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 求肥|1=gyūhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109866}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>
Kirizanshō dates back to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and is said to have been favored by the tea master [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]].<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive [[:en:Counting rods|sangoku]] shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>

{{ill|Kirizanshō|ja|切山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizanshō|1=kirizashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682544}} is a type of [[:en:Wagashi|wagashi]] (Japanese confectionery) traditionally eaten during the [[:en:Japanese New Year|Japanese New Year]]. It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko (rice flour)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上新粉|1=jōshinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359063}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンショウ|1=Zanthoxylum piperitum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167881}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a [[:en:Usu (mortar)|mortar and pestle]], and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling [[:en:Counting rods|Sangoku]] or [[:en:Hyōshigi|hyōshigi]] (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016th year 7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/> 

The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red and white (Japan)|1=red and white|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90812259}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matcha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 抹茶|1=matcha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q822331}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒砂糖|1=black sugar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135455243}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998th year 、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerizanshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gyūhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 求肥|1=gyūhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109866}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>

Kirizanshō dates back to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and is said to have been favored by the tea master [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]].<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive [[:en:Counting rods|sangoku]] shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>

In [[:en:Haikai|haikai]], Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ([[:en:Kigo|kigo]]) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> 
{{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō''  
[[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}

It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko (rice flour)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上新粉|1=jōshinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359063}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンショウ|1=Zanthoxylum piperitum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167881}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a [[:en:Usu (mortar)|mortar and pestle]], and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling [[:en:Counting rods|Sangoku]] or [[:en:Hyōshigi|hyōshigi]] (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016th year 7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/> 

The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red and white (Japan)|1=red and white|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90812259}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matcha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 抹茶|1=matcha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q822331}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒砂糖|1=black sugar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135455243}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998th year 、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerizanshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gyūhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 求肥|1=gyūhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109866}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>

Kirizanshō dates back to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and is said to have been favored by the tea master [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]].<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive [[:en:Counting rods|sangoku]] shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>

In [[:en:Haikai|haikai]], Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ([[:en:Kigo|kigo]]) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> 
{{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō''  
[[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}

The distinctive {{ill|Counting rods|en|Counting rods|WD=Q583473|lt=sangoku}} shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28682544]] Wikidata name: kirizashō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28682544]] Wikidata name: kirizashō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Kirizanshō|ja|切山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizanshō|1=kirizashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682544}} is a type of {{ill|Wagashi|en|Wagashi|WD=Q1063096|lt=wagashi}} (Japanese confectionery) traditionally eaten during the {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=Japanese New Year}}. It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko (rice flour)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上新粉|1=jōshinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359063}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンショウ|1=Zanthoxylum piperitum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167881}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a {{ill|Usu (mortar)|en|Usu (mortar)|WD=Q268518|lt=mortar and pestle}}, and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling {{ill|Counting rods|en|Counting rods|WD=Q583473|lt=Sangoku}} or {{ill|Hyōshigi|en|Hyōshigi|WD=Q3144475|lt=hyōshigi}} (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016th year 7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>
The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red and white (Japan)|1=red and white|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90812259}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matcha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 抹茶|1=matcha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q822331}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒砂糖|1=black sugar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135455243}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998th year 、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerizanshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gyūhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 求肥|1=gyūhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109866}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>
Kirizanshō dates back to the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} and is said to have been favored by the tea master {{ill|Kobori Enshū|en|Kobori Enshū|WD=Q541040|lt=Kobori Enshū}}.<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive {{ill|Counting rods|en|Counting rods|WD=Q583473|lt=sangoku}} shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>
In {{ill|Haikai|en|Haikai|WD=Q17049321|lt=haikai}}, Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ({{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|WD=Q1085881|lt=kigo}}) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> {{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō'' [[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsuruoka no Kirisansho|ja|鶴岡のきりさんしょ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruoka no Kirisansho|1=kirisansho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65293815}}
{{food-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]]
{{ill|Kirizanshō|ja|切山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizanshō|1=Kirizansho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682544}} is a type of {{ill|Wagashi|en|Wagashi|WD=Q1063096|lt=wagashi|1=Wagashi}} (Japanese confectionery) traditionally eaten during the {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=Japanese New Year|1=Japanese New Year}}. It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko (rice flour)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上新粉|1=jōshinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359063}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンショウ|1=Zanthoxylum piperitum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167881}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a {{ill|Usu (mortar)|en|Usu (mortar)|WD=Q268518|lt=mortar and pestle|1=Usu (mortar)}}, and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling {{ill|Counting rods|en|Counting rods|WD=Q583473|lt=Sangoku|1=Counting rods}} or {{ill|Hyōshigi|en|Hyōshigi|WD=Q3144475|lt=hyōshigi|1=Hyōshigi}} (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016th year 7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>
The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red and white (Japan)|1=red and white|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90812259}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matcha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 抹茶|1=Matcha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q822331}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒砂糖|1=black sugar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135455243}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998th year 、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerizanshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gyūhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 求肥|1=Gyūhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109866}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>
Kirizanshō dates back to the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} and is said to have been favored by the tea master {{ill|Kobori Enshū|en|Kobori Enshū|WD=Q541040|lt=Kobori Enshū|1=Kobori Enshū}}.<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive {{ill|Counting rods|en|Counting rods|WD=Q583473|lt=sangoku|1=Counting rods}} shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>
In {{ill|Haikai|en|Haikai|WD=Q17049321|lt=haikai|1=Haikai}}, Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ({{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|WD=Q1085881|lt=kigo|1=Kigo}}) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> {{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō'' [[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsuruoka no Kirisansho|ja|鶴岡のきりさんしょ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruoka no Kirisansho|1=kirisansho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65293815}}
{{food-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:切山椒]]
{{ill|Kirizanshō|ja|切山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizanshō|1=Kirizansho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28682544}} is a type of {{ill|Wagashi|en|Wagashi|qid=Q1063096|lt=wagashi|1=Wagashi}} (Japanese confectionery) traditionally eaten during the {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|qid=Q701398|lt=Japanese New Year|1=Japanese New Year}}. It is a rice cake confection made by mixing {{ill|Shinko (rice flour)|ja|糝粉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko (rice flour)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上新粉|1=jōshinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359063}}, sugar, and ground {{ill|sanshō|ja|山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンショウ|1=Zanthoxylum piperitum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q167881}} (Japanese pepper), or sometimes the essence extracted from sanshō in lukewarm water. The mixture is kneaded into dough, steamed, pounded with a {{ill|Usu (mortar)|en|Usu (mortar)|qid=Q268518|lt=mortar and pestle|1=Usu (mortar)}}, and then cut into long rectangular shapes resembling {{ill|Counting rods|en|Counting rods|qid=Q583473|lt=Sangoku|1=Counting rods}} or {{ill|Hyōshigi|en|Hyōshigi|qid=Q3144475|lt=hyōshigi|1=Hyōshigi}} (wooden clappers).<ref name="沢">沢史生 「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%88%87%E5%B1%B1%E6%A4%92-480237#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 Kirizanshō]」 日本大百科全書、2016th year 7月25日閲覧。</ref><ref name="山本"/>
The confection is often dyed in {{ill|Red and white (Japan)|ja|紅白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red and white (Japan)|1=red and white|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q90812259}}, but five-colored versions using {{ill|matcha|ja|碾茶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matcha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 抹茶|1=Matcha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q822331}} or {{ill|kokutō|ja|黒糖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黒砂糖|1=black sugar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q135455243}} are also available.<ref name="沢"/><ref name="春樹">角川春樹編 『合本 現代俳句歳時記』 角川春樹事務所、1998th year 、1328-1329頁。</ref> A more luxurious variety, called {{ill|Nerizanshō|ja|練山椒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerizanshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is made by further steaming and kneading the dough after it is pounded, producing a premium {{ill|gyūhi|ja|ぎゅうひ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gyūhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 求肥|1=Gyūhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109866}}-style confection.<ref name="沢"/>
Kirizanshō dates back to the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} and is said to have been favored by the tea master {{ill|Kobori Enshū|en|Kobori Enshū|qid=Q541040|lt=Kobori Enshū|1=Kobori Enshū}}.<ref name="沢"/> It was originally called sanshō mochi (山椒餅). The distinctive {{ill|Counting rods|en|Counting rods|qid=Q583473|lt=sangoku|1=Counting rods}} shape became common during the late Edo period.<ref name="山本">山本候充編 『百菓辞典』 東京堂出版、1997th year 、71-72頁。</ref> Additionally, it is sold as a lucky charm at the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival.<ref>[[小林信彦]] 『にっちもさっちも <small>人生は五十一から 5</small>』 [[文春文庫]] ［こ-6-18］ ISBN 978-4167256180、278-279p</ref><ref>小林信彦 『映画の話が多くなって <small>本音を申せば 9</small>』 文春文庫 ［こ-6-35］ ISBN 978-4167905354、245p</ref>
In {{ill|Haikai|en|Haikai|qid=Q17049321|lt=haikai|1=Haikai}}, Kirizanshō is also treated as a seasonal word ({{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|qid=Q1085881|lt=kigo|1=Kigo}}) for the New Year.<ref name="春樹"/> {{quote|''Wakakusa no iro mo soetari kirizanshō'' [[Kubota Mantarō|久保田万太郎}}
